children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may use the authorities set forth in this section with respect to any procurement made during the period beginning on the effective date of this chapter and ending , if the Secretary determines in writing that the mission of the Department (as described in ) would be seriously impaired without the use of such authorities.September 30, 2007section 111 of this title

children.heading: Delegation
children.label: 2 Delegation
children.text: The authority to make the determination described in paragraph (1) may not be delegated by the Secretary to an officer of the Department who is not appointed by the President with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: Increased micro-purchase threshold for certain procurements
children.label: b Increased micro-purchase threshold for certain procurements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may designate certain employees of the Department to make procurements described in subsection (a) for which in the administration of the amount specified in subsections (a), (d), and (e) of such section 1902 shall be deemed to be $7,500.section 1902 of title 41

children.label: A
children.text: fewer than the number of employees of the Department who are authorized to make purchases without obtaining competitive quotations, pursuant to ;section 1902(d) of title 41

children.label: B
children.text: sufficient to ensure the geographic dispersal of the availability of the use of the procurement authority under such paragraph at locations reasonably considered to be potential terrorist targets; and

children.label: C
children.text: sufficiently limited to allow for the careful monitoring of employees designated under such paragraph.

children.heading: Review
children.label: 3 Review
children.text: Procurements made under the authority of this subsection shall be subject to review by a designated supervisor on not less than a monthly basis. The supervisor responsible for the review shall be responsible for no more than 7 employees making procurements under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a contract to be awarded and performed, or purchase to be made, within the United States, $200,000; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a contract to be awarded and performed, or purchase to be made, outside of the United States, $300,000.

children.heading: Application of certain commercial items authorities
children.label: d Application of certain commercial items authorities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to a procurement described in subsection (a), the Secretary may deem any item or service to be a commercial item for the purpose of Federal procurement laws.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The $5,000,000 limitation provided in and shall be deemed to be $7,500,000 for purposes of property or services under the authority of this subsection.section 1901(a)(2) of title 41section 3305(a)(2) of title 41

children.heading: Certain authority
children.label: 3 Certain authority
children.text: Authority under a provision of law referred to in paragraph (2) that expires under section 4202(e) of the Clinger-Cohen Act of 1996 (divisions D and E of ; note) shall, notwithstanding such section, continue to apply for a procurement described in subsection (a).Public Law 104–10610 U.S.C. 2304

An assessment of the extent to which property and services acquired using authorities provided under this section contributed to the capacity of the Federal workforce to facilitate the mission of the Department as described in .section 111 of this title
An assessment of the extent to which prices for property and services acquired using authorities provided under this section reflected the best value.
The number of employees designated by each executive agency under subsection (b)(1).
An assessment of the extent to which the Department has implemented subsections (b)(2) and (b)(3) to monitor the use of procurement authority by employees designated under subsection (b)(1).
Any recommendations of the Comptroller General for improving the effectiveness of the implementation of the provisions of this section.
